sutta: now the vulnerable sariputta came to visit the exalted 1 as he said at 1 side the venerable sariputta said this to the exalted 1 pray lord what is the reason what is the cause why such and such a trait practiced by some person turns out a failure again lord what is the reason what is the cause why such and such a trait practiced by some person turns not does not turn out as he intended pray lord what is the reason why such and such a trait turns out as he intended again lord what is the reason what is the cause why such and such a trait practiced by some person prospers beyond his intention [Music] and then the buddha said in this matter sariputta suppose someone comes to a recluse or brahman and makes him an offer saying sir say what you want by way of support but he does not give him what he offered if this man diseases from that life and returns to this sort of life whatever trade he may practice it turns out a failure again in this matter sariputta suppose someone comes to a recluse or brahman and makes him an offer saying sir say what you want by way of support but he does not give him what he intended to give then if this man diseases from that life and returns to this sort of life whatever trait he may practice it does not turn out as he intended then again sariputta suppose someone comes to a recluse of brahman and makes him a similar offer and gives him what he intended to give then if this man diseases and come comes back to the human world whatever trade he may practice turns out as he intended once more sorry putta suppose someone comes to a recluse or brahman and makes him a similar offer and gives him more than he intended to give then if this man diseases from that life and returns to this sort of life whatever trait he may practice it prospers beyond his intention this sariputta is the reason this is the cause why such and such a trait practiced by some person turns out a failure or does not turn out as he intended or does does turn out as he intended or prospers beyond his intention that's the end of the suta
commentary: here the buddha is talking about this a person who does a trade that means i open some business and when he opens he does some business he has some expectation of a certain profit that he hopes to make or he he thinks that he should make a certain profit but the result can come out 4 ways 1 is uh it turns out a miserable failure maybe becomes bankrupt second 1 maybe expects to make say for example 100 dollars a month but he does not make as much as he intended maybe he gets only 50 dollars a month he expected to get 100 dollars but he got only 50 dollars that's the second case the third case is he expects to make a 100 dollars a month and he actually makes a 100 dollars a month as a third case the fourth case is even though he expects to make a 100 dollars a month he might make 200 a month uh so this is uh quite a [Music] quite a valid uh thing in life like you see sometimes 2 shops on the same road on the same street selling the same goods 1 shop might do very well another shop might not do well or sometimes somebody opens a shop and then he goes bankrupt and then he sells off the shop another person comes back and a second person comes takes over the same shop and maybe does the same business and then he prospers i see each person's karma is different so the reason the buddha said is because of somebody promising to give something and whether he gives or not therefore 4 possibilities the first 1 uh he says he intends to give something to a virtuous uh to a virtuous uh this uh recluse or brahmin that means a renuncian among he says he promises to to give something and then in the end he does not give anything at all so a result of that is when karma ripens in the same way he does a business he intends to make something he doesn't make anything and he goes bankrupt so the second case is a person promises to give something to a virtuous monk and then he gives less than what he promised to give so the result is that when he does a business he also gets less than what he intended to get the third 1 he promised something and he gave exactly what he promised so when he does the business the returns is also exactly what he expected the fourth 1 he said he wanted to give something but he gave much more to the virtuous manga and gave much more than he intended to give so in the same way when he does uh in a later life when he does business then uh he gets much more profit than he thought he was going to get so this suta shows that we should not be careless with our words and our promises a perhaps if you make a promise to a person who is not virtuous who is not [Music] maybe has a lot of bad karma then the result won't be so bad for you but if you in dealings with a virtuous person now you have to be very careful because if you do wrong to a virtuous person then the form your karma is much worse than if to an ordinary person the next utah is
